content: Just as human language learners use lexical reflection (the common linguistic understanding of reflexion) to internalize word usage and correct misinterpretations, LLMs can integrate this structured, vocabulary-aligned reflective step into the Self-Refine iterative feedback framework to improve structured output like technical writing or domain-specific documentation. This hybrid framework leverages reflexion’s core function of grounding meaning in learned context to fix the common Self-Refine flaw of drifting away from required domain terminology conventions.
